title: GPDE Workshop - Regularity results for non-local equations
speaker: Luis Silvestre

proof_stages.label: Probabilistic origin and formulation of nonlocal equations
proof_stages.summary: The talk identifies the target class as fully nonlinear integro-differential equations arising from stochastic control of jump processes, with positive symmetric kernels, nonlocal exterior Dirichlet data, and viscosity well-posedness in the background.

proof_stages.label: Second-order elliptic benchmark theory
proof_stages.summary: The speaker reviews uniform ellipticity for fully nonlinear second-order PDE, Pucci extremal operators, the Krylov-Safonov Harnack/Hölder theory, and the Evans-Krylov theorem as the results to be reproduced nonlocally.

proof_stages.label: Nonlocal uniform ellipticity through order-sigma extremal operators
proof_stages.summary: The local Pucci comparison condition is replaced by extremal operators formed from all symmetric kernels comparable to the fractional-Laplacian kernel, giving the structural ellipticity assumption for the nonlocal theory.

proof_stages.label: Nonlocal regularity theorem statements
proof_stages.summary: Under the nonlocal ellipticity condition, the talk states analogues of Harnack and Hölder estimates, C1-alpha regularity for general nonlinear equations, and C-sigma-plus-alpha regularity for convex or concave equations, with constants stable as sigma tends to two.

proof_stages.label: Nonlocal substitute for the ABP estimate
proof_stages.summary: The proof of the Harnack theory is reduced to an ABP-type contact-set estimate in which the nonlocal extremal inequality controls how much a function can separate from its supporting plane on dyadic rings, yielding a measure-to-pointwise estimate.

proof_stages.label: Difference and incremental-quotient mechanism for C1-alpha regularity
proof_stages.summary: Ellipticity implies that differences of solutions, and hence incremental quotients, satisfy the extremal inequalities, so the nonlocal Hölder estimates can be applied to obtain C1-alpha regularity after controlling tail errors.

proof_stages.label: Concavity ingredients for the nonlocal Evans-Krylov theorem
proof_stages.summary: For concave operators, averages/translates and linear integro-differential operators applied to solutions give subsolutions, while positive and negative parts of the nonlocal increments control each other, leading to a two-step proof of absolute convergence and then C-sigma-plus-alpha regularity.
